steps.title: Identify the clauses
steps.content: Before the break, the sentence completes one independent clause: "the guide soon grew in scope and influence." After the break, "its modest French eateries were replaced with some of the most esteemed restaurants" begins another independent clause.

steps.title: Join independent clauses
steps.content: Choice A gives only a comma, causing a comma splice. Choice B gives the conjunction "and" without the comma normally needed between two independent clauses, and Choice D gives no connector at all. Choice C supplies comma plus coordinating conjunction, correctly joining the two clauses. The answer is C.
